text:
```ex zentoring/lib/app_web/components/social_auth_buttons.ex defmodule AppWeb.Components.SocialAuthButtons do use Phoenix.Component use Phoenix.HTML attr :class, :string, default: nil attr :return_to, :string, default: "/" def social_buttons(assigns) do ~H"""
<.google_button return_to={@return_to} /> <.apple_button return_to={@return_to} />
""" end attr :return_to, :string, required: true def google_button(assigns) do ~H""" Continue with Google """ end attr :return_to, :string, required: true def apple_button(assigns) do ~H""" Continue with Apple """ end end ``` diagnostics zentoring/priv/repo/migrations/[TIMESTAMP]_add_social_auth_fields.exs ```elixir defmodule App.Repo.Migrations.AddSocialAuthFields do // error: ** (TokenMissingError) token missing on /Users/irwin/Work/perso/dev/zentoring/priv/repo/migrations/[TIMESTAMP]_add_social_auth_fields.exs:25:4: // error: missing terminator: end // hint: it looks like the "do" on line 1 does not have a matching "end" // │ // 1 │ defmodule App.Repo.Migrations.AddSocialAuthFields do // │ └ unclosed delimiter // ... // 25 │ end // │ └ missing closing delimiter (expected "end") // │ // └─ /Users/irwin/Work/perso/dev/zentoring/priv/repo/migrations/[TIMESTAMP]_add_social_auth_fields.exs:25:4 use Ecto.Migration def change do alter table(:users) do add :google_id, :string add :apple_id, :string end create index(:users, [:google_id]) create index(:users, [:apple_id]) end end ``` zentoring/lib/app_web/components/social_auth_buttons.ex ```elixir defmodule AppWeb.Components.SocialAuthButtons do use Phoenix.Component use Phoenix.HTML // error: ** (RuntimeError) use Phoenix.HTML is no longer supported in v4.0. // // To keep compatibility with previous versions, add {:phoenix_html_helpers, "~> 1.0"} to your mix.exs deps // and then, instead of "use Phoenix.HTML", you might: // // import Phoenix.HTML // import Phoenix.HTML.Form // use PhoenixHTMLHelpers attr :class, :string, default: nil ``` correct error socialbuttons Based on the diagnostics, there are two issues to fix: 1. For `social_auth_buttons.ex`, we need to update the Phoenix.HTML usage for Phoenix 1.7/2.0. Here's the corrected version: ```elixir defmodule AppWeb.Components.SocialAuthButtons do use Phoenix.Component import Phoenix.HTML import Phoenix.HTML.Form use PhoenixHTMLHelpers attr :class, :string, default: nil attr :return_to, :string, default: "/" def social_buttons(assigns) do ~H"""
<.google_button return_to={@return_to} /> <.apple_button return_to={@return_to} />
""" end attr :return_to, :string, required: true def google_button(assigns) do ~H""" Continue with Google """ end attr :return_to, :string, required: true def apple_button(assigns) do ~H""" Continue with Apple """ end end ``` 2. You'll also need to add the `phoenix_html_helpers` dependency to your `mix.exs`: ```elixir defp deps do [ # ... other deps {:phoenix_html_helpers, "~> 1.0"} ] end ``` After adding the dependency, run: ```bash mix deps.get ``` These changes should resolve the compilation errors you're seeing. The migration file looks correct and doesn't need any changes. diagnostics zentoring/priv/repo/migrations/[TIMESTAMP]_add_social_auth_fields.exs ```elixir defmodule App.Repo.Migrations.AddSocialAuthFields do // error: ** (TokenMissingError) token missing on /Users/irwin/Work/perso/dev/zentoring/priv/repo/migrations/[TIMESTAMP]_add_social_auth_fields.exs:25:4: // error: missing terminator: end // hint: it looks like the "do" on line 1 does not have a matching "end" // │ // 1 │ defmodule App.Repo.Migrations.AddSocialAuthFields do // │ └ unclosed delimiter // ... // 25 │ end // │ └ missing closing delimiter (expected "end") // │ // └─ /Users/irwin/Work/perso/dev/zentoring/priv/repo/migrations/[TIMESTAMP]_add_social_auth_fields.exs:25:4 use Ecto.Migration def change do alter table(:users) do add :google_id, :string add :apple_id, :string end create index(:users, [:google_id]) create index(:users, [:apple_id]) end end ``` zentoring/lib/app_web/live/user_login_live.ex ```elixir defmodule AppWeb.UserLoginLive do // error: ** (CompileError) lib/app_web/live/user_login_live.ex: cannot compile module AppWeb.UserLoginLive (errors have been logged) use AppWeb, :live_view alias AppWeb.Components.SocialAuthButtons ``` ```elixir <.header class="text-center"> Log in to account <.social_buttons class="mt-6" return_to={@return_to} /> // error: undefined function social_buttons/1 (expected AppWeb.UserLoginLive to define such a function or for it to be imported, but none are available)
``` zentoring/lib/app_web/components/social_auth_buttons.ex ```elixir ~H""" ``` ```elixir ~H""" ``` ```ex zentoring/lib/app_web/components/social_auth_buttons.ex defmodule AppWeb.Components.SocialAuthButtons do use Phoenix.Component import Phoenix.HTML import Phoenix.HTML.Form use PhoenixHTMLHelpers attr :class, :string, default: nil attr :return_to, :string, default: "/" def social_buttons(assigns) do ~H"""
<.google_button return_to={@return_to} /> <.apple_button return_to={@return_to} />
""" end attr :return_to, :string, required: true def google_button(assigns) do ~H""" Continue with Google """ end attr :return_to, :string, required: true def apple_button(assigns) do ~H""" Continue with Apple """ end end ``` correct sigil error Here are the corrections needed: 1. First, add the path helpers import to `social_auth_buttons.ex`: ```elixir defmodule AppWeb.Components.SocialAuthButtons do use Phoenix.Component import Phoenix.HTML import Phoenix.HTML.Form use PhoenixHTMLHelpers # Add this line to import path helpers import AppWeb.CoreComponents # ... rest of the code remains the same end ``` 2. For the user login live view, update it to use the social buttons component: ```elixir defmodule AppWeb.UserLoginLive do use AppWeb, :live_view alias AppWeb.Components.SocialAuthButtons # Add this import to use the component import SocialAuthButtons # ... in your template: # Change .social_buttons to .social_buttons <.social_buttons class="mt-6" return_to={@return_to} /> ``` The main issues were: 1. Missing path helper imports for the `~p` sigil 2. Component naming mismatch (use `social_buttons` instead of `social_buttons`) 3. Proper importing of the component in the login live view Make sure your `AppWeb` module (typically in `lib/app_web.ex`) properly defines the core components and router helpers in the `:live_view` block: ```elixir def live_view do quote do use Phoenix.LiveView, layout: {AppWeb.Layouts, :app} # Make sure these are included import AppWeb.CoreComponents unquote(html_helpers()) end end ``` These changes should resolve the sigil errors and component usage issues.
